label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out if following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. Let me break it down step by step. First, the instructions say to always face forward. So my direction doesn't change during the steps. Let's assume I start facing North. That's a common convention, right? So my initial direction is North. Now, the steps are: 1. Take 3 steps right. 2. Take 1 step backward. 3. Take 6 steps left. 4. Take 1 step forward. 5. Take 3 steps right. Let me track my position and direction after each step. Starting position: (0, 0), facing North. 1. Take 3 steps right. If I'm facing North, right would be East. So moving 3 steps to the East. My new position is (3, 0), still facing North. 2. Take 1 step backward. Facing North, backward is South. So I move 1 step South from (3, 0) to (3, -1), still facing North. 3. Take 6 steps left. Left of North is West. So moving 6 steps West from (3, -1). Each step West decreases the x-coordinate. 3 - 6 = -3. So new position is (-3, -1), still facing North. 4. Take 1 step forward. Facing North, forward is North. So from (-3, -1), moving 1 step North to (-3, 0), still facing North. 5. Take 3 steps right. Again, right of North is East. So from (-3, 0), moving 3 steps East. Each step East increases the x-coordinate. -3 + 3 = 0. So new position is (0, 0), still facing North. Wait, so after all these steps, I end up back at (0, 0), which is the starting point. So the answer should be Yes, right? Let me double-check each step to make sure I didn't make an error in direction or calculation. Starting at (0,0), facing North. 1. 3 right (East): (3,0) - correct. 2. 1 backward (South): (3,-1)
